extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the Madrid street circuit sprint after Deceuninck-Quick-Step controlled pace at 69+ km/h through penultimate corner
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck positioned 4 riders at front to string out peloton at 69-71 km/h before hairpin corner, eliminating positioning battles
CAVAGNA Rémy executed pace-killing turn into hairpin precisely where instructed, swinging off immediately after to deploy fresh domestiques
MAJKA Rafael repeatedly led out at front for UAE despite being climber, suggesting team lacked sprint train depth

rider_signals.MAJKA Rafael: neutral: Polish climber twice leading peloton on flat circuit reveals UAE's weak sprint support infrastructure
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: won second stage via photo finish against BENNETT, claims this most important career victory at age 22
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: boxed in final 500m despite Quick-Step's obvious positioning strategy, second consecutive photo loss to Jakobsen
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémy: positive: sustained 71 km/h leadout into hairpin while visibly suffering, executed tactical instruction perfectly before swinging off
